intro: Thelypteris × jerdonii, also known as the Jerdon's maiden fern, is a natural hybrid fern species that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris × jerdonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris × jerdonii is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate inflammation.
